title: the hour without pulse
essence: drag a scalpel across the body of a single day and excise the moments that were never yours.
description: An operating theatre rendered in clinical white. The patient on the table is one Tuesday — diagrammed as an hourglass body, its interior sown with nineteen moments classified as tumor, tendon, or bone. The user takes the scalpel-cursor and drags. Each cut leaves a permanent suture, a faint bleed, and a lighter day. The pulse line of the body responds to what is removed. There is no clean undo; sutures restore tendons but the scar remains.
interaction_summary: Drag the scalpel-cursor across the body to excise moments — tumors are wasted hours, tendons are load-bearing time; every cut leaves a permanent scar.
secrets: excise the noon tendon (10:45) to release a faint blue ghost — a tendon's hollow filled with weather
excise the moment at 23:58, 'the hour without pulse', and the patient's pulse line flatlines
excise two or more 'meeting' moments to surface the hidden sentence about scheduled hours
press R after cutting a tendon to suture it back — the moment returns, but the scar does not
press F to file the chart and read the post-operative verdict

wake_log.event: first sketch — a literal body on a table felt gory; pivoted to an hourglass form whose interior is the day.
the cut nearly didn't read as surgery — added cross-hatched sutures perpendicular to the path, then tiny blood-bead deposits along the seam, and the metaphor finally landed.
kept the irreversible scar (sutures restore the moment but never the wound), abandoned an early gore-meter, replaced it with 'integrity %' as the body's verdict on the surgeon.
